 public void go() {
 JFrame frame = new JFrame(); 
 JPanel panel = new JPanel();
 panel.setBackground(Color.darkGray); 
 JButton button = new JButton(“shock me”);
 panel.add(button);
 frame.getContentPane().add(BorderLayout.EAST, panel);
 frame.setSize(250,200);
 frame.setVisible(true);
 }
Let’5 add a button to the panel
Add the button to the panel and add the 
panel to the frame. The panel’5 layout manager 
(flow) controls the button, and the frame’5 
layout manager (border) controls the panel.
The panel expanded!
And the button got its 
preferred size in both 
dimensions, because the panel 
uses flow layout, and the 
button is part of the panel 
(not the frame).

 public void go() {

 panel.setBackground(Color.darkGray); 
 JButton button = new JButton(“shock me”);
 panel.add(button);
 frame.getContentPane().add(BorderLayout.EAST, panel);
 frame.setSize(250,200);
 frame.setVisible(true);
 }
